109043.explanation: We believe a positive view should be taken on the possibility of such activities at farms. But not everything can always and everywhere happen. There must be a balanced consideration of interests with the surroundings.

109045.explanation: Large distribution centers and box-type buildings do not fit in our open municipality.

109046.explanation: Shelter in a rural area with limited public transport is far from ideal. If a dispersal law applies in the Netherlands, Altena must also take responsibility. We then choose small-scale location(s) with good coordination with the surroundings being crucial.

109047.explanation: If there is a limited number of workers, this is already possible. For larger groups of labor migrants, we believe in a central, well-organized housing of a maximum of two hundred labor migrants working in Altena, like the one currently at Kromme Nol in Wijk en Aalburg.

109049.explanation: For many people, a wood stove is a source of coziness and warmth. We do not support bans. However, we do support good information to prevent wood stove burning on calm, foggy days and the use of wrong materials as much as possible.

109051.explanation: In Altena with 21 villages, it is not feasible to organize one central fireworks event. The municipality should cooperate positively with initiatives from villages for pleasant, central New Year’s Eve celebrations where centralized and organized fireworks can also be set off.

109055.explanation: We see the countryside and agricultural land primarily as places providing our food supply. We prefer faster building in and near existing villages with equal conditions applying to all landowners.

109059.explanation: Music education is important for a child’s development. For parents who cannot afford this, a contribution is desirable.

109060.explanation: Financial contributions should not be the first choice. Together with sports federations and possibly the municipality’s association supporter, first look at viability, what needs or can be changed, and lastly consider financial support.

109063.explanation: CDA Altena values safety and livability: enforcement officers (boa’s) are crucial. They are the point of contact in the neighborhood, ensure enforcement, and contribute to a sense of order and calm. We want residents to feel safe and rules to be fairly enforced.

109064.explanation: CDA Altena wants residents to feel safe in their own environment. Camera surveillance in public spaces can help prevent and detect crime and vandalism but must always be used carefully and temporarily.

109066.explanation: CDA Altena wants youth and starters to remain able to live in our municipality. For many starters, buying a first home is difficult due to high prices and mortgage rules. A starter’s loan provides that extra financial boost to buy their own home.

109069.explanation: We are basically against structural permanent residence in holiday homes. It prevents blight, safety risks, and speculation. CDA Altena chooses real homes as the solution to the housing shortage. We make an exception at Veense Put because a long-term process is already underway there.

109071.explanation: CDA Altena supports the idea that people on social assistance benefits should not sit passively at home but actively participate in society as much as possible. However, a strict obligation seems neither feasible nor desirable.

109073.explanation: Poverty reduction must reach those who really need it. A higher threshold can lead to dispersal of resources and less support for the most vulnerable.

109076.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases traffic safety for pedestrians and cyclists and contributes to a livable residential environment. Lower speed results in fewer traffic victims and less noise nuisance and more room for vulnerable road users. On through roads, 50 km/h is fine.

109077.explanation: Care should align as much as possible with a person’s background. At the same time, there are limits: care must always remain safe, professional, and feasible. It must not lead to exclusion, lower quality, or substantially higher costs.

109079.explanation: CDA Altena believes that property tax should not increase beyond inflation.

109083.explanation: We are against standard Sunday openings of supermarkets and thus also against shopping Sundays. Large chains like Albert Heijn and Jumbo want more turnover, but this harms small stores and local entrepreneurs in Altena. CDA Altena wants to protect these shops for livability.

109084.explanation: Refugees with residence permits integrate best when they have independent housing. The municipality of Altena, together with housing corporations, meets the national quotas to house a very limited number of status holders in Altena.

109092.explanation: Renting out individual rooms is an emergency measure and does not provide a sustainable solution. CDA Altena wants to focus on real homes and apartments for starters, families, and seniors.

109094.explanation: In most villages in Altena there is no structural shortage of parking spaces. Adding extra parking spots unnecessarily occupies public space and can come at the expense of greenery and livability.

109096.explanation: Such a large plan, attracting many visitors from outside Altena weekly, does not fit in the rural environment of Dussen. Activities possible within the current zoning could, however, be allowed.

109097.explanation: Young people need places to meet each other. Entertainment venues contribute to a sense of community and prevent young people from becoming isolated. Foundations such as the Soos and de Pomp in Almkerk deserve support. Commercial hospitality should be self-sustaining.

109098.explanation: CDA Altena believes that new residential neighborhoods must be not only sustainable but also livable. Trees and greenery contribute to a healthy living environment, reduce heat stress, and provide a pleasant place to live and socialize.

109099.explanation: Additional bus lines improve the accessibility of villages and amenities, especially for residents without a car, such as young people, the elderly, and people with disabilities. Good public transport increases equal opportunities, reduces traffic pressure, and contributes to more sustainable transport.

109043.explanation: According to AltenaLokaal, Always goes too far, but depending on the situation, consultation about it is always possible.

109045.explanation: All traffic nuisances and migrant workers who also cause social unrest are reasons for AltenaLokaal to oppose this.

109046.explanation: Our municipality cannot handle this process.

109047.explanation: AltenaLokaal follows the advice of the Roemer commission here: there must be a separation between the employment contract and other rights such as housing.

109049.explanation: We see this as the individual responsibility of our residents.

109051.explanation: The municipality should not do that. There is a national fireworks ban. AltenaLokaal wants to look for appropriate alternatives.

109055.explanation: Provided that one complies with our policy: AltenaLokaal is always willing to think along.

109059.explanation: We have great diversity in Altena so that our young people can develop both in sports and music.

109060.explanation: AltenaLokaal wants vital associations and thinks along about how structural improvements can be made.

109063.explanation: Safety is a spearhead of AltenaLokaal and community officers can enforce on minor annoyances that the police leave aside.

109064.explanation: At designated locations based on good information.

109066.explanation: AltenaLokaal wants to increase the chances of housing for young people in this way.

109069.explanation: We receive money from the national government for permanent homes, but not for holiday homes.

109071.explanation: Everyone should contribute to our society within their own capabilities.

109073.explanation: The municipality has creative options for customized solutions to help those who need it most (special assistance).

109077.explanation: Altena has a unique character where we want to offer everyone the care that suits their needs.

109079.explanation: The property tax (ozb) may only be increased if appropriate facilities are provided in return.

109083.explanation: AltenaLokaal supports deregulation. It is up to the entrepreneur whether to take advantage of it.

109084.explanation: We follow the legislation in this regard.

109092.explanation: Emergency accommodation must be possible both for starters and for urgent seekers.

109094.explanation: More encouragement is needed for alternative forms of mobility.

109096.explanation: Given our character in Altena, this is not a religious denomination that fits.

109097.explanation: That is not a municipal task. Facilitating it is.

109099.explanation: It is a provincial task to ensure an adequate bus connection. In practice, this is insufficiently possible. Therefore, the municipality must arrange bus transport itself in the core areas.

109043.explanation: The SGP has a positive attitude but it cannot be everywhere and always. It must be assessed per situation.

109045.explanation: SGP Altena finds distribution centers unsuitable in the rural area of the Altena municipality.

109046.explanation: Performing statutory tasks within the frameworks established by the municipal council.

109047.explanation: Provided they are small-scale, meet quality standards, and are only for seasonal workers employed by the respective company.

109049.explanation: It is important to look at how to limit nuisance as much as possible.

109051.explanation: SGP Altena supports a fireworks-free Altena.

109055.explanation: Together with farmers who are stopping, tailor-made solutions must be sought.

109059.explanation: SGP Altena supports the reintroduction of subsidies for individual music education.

109060.explanation: Sports clubs have their own responsibility to generate sufficient income.

109063.explanation: If the need arises, an increase in the number of community officers is justified.

109064.explanation: Cameras should be deployed incidentally in case of problems. Good lighting and open structures also increase safety.

109066.explanation: The current situation must be maintained.

109069.explanation: This is not appropriate within the applicable zoning plans.

109071.explanation: SGP Altena is in favor of reciprocity.

109073.explanation: The poverty line is determined based on national standards.

109076.explanation: SGP Altena believes this can vary per situation and therefore the applicable maximum speed should be considered per village.

109079.explanation: SGP Altena believes the municipality should first critically review its own expenditures before considering an increase in property tax (ozb).

109083.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest in the Christian tradition; this day is meant to honor and serve God. SGP Altena wants to preserve this.

109084.explanation: The municipality must treat refugees mercifully, but this cannot be at the expense of our own residents.

109092.explanation: Not in favor. However, SGP Altena has a positive attitude towards splitting houses.

109094.explanation: This must be examined and evaluated per area.

109096.explanation: Evaluate based on Spatial Planning regulations.

109097.explanation: SGP Altena does not see it as the municipality’s task to invest in nightlife options for young people.

109098.explanation: Sufficient nature and shade are important for livability and climate adaptation.

109099.explanation: The municipality must actively collaborate with the province to think about alternative transportation options in our region.

109043.explanation: Side activities help farmers maintain a healthy income. We must pay attention to livability, nature, and traffic safety so that it remains pleasant for everyone. The connection between farms and our villages is valuable and deserves protection.

109045.explanation: We prefer companies that rapidly phase out fossil energy in our municipality. Sustainability, circularity, and local employment are therefore important criteria. Companies mainly dependent on migrant labor (such as distribution centers) do not get priority.

109046.explanation: We shelter people fleeing war and violence, as agreed with the province. We remain hospitable and ensure asylum seekers and status holders can quickly participate through work, sports, and social contact.

109047.explanation: It is important that seasonal workers have good housing and that any nuisance is prevented. Whether this housing should be located on the employer’s site depends on the local situation.

109049.explanation: Wood burning can cause nuisance. It is therefore important to burn consciously and use existing advice from institutions such as the GGD, MilieuCentraal, and RIVM to support careful choices.

109051.explanation: We want every village to have the opportunity to hold a New Year's Eve party for all residents, featuring a communal fireworks show to ring in the new year.

109055.explanation: Agricultural land that becomes available should, where possible, be designated for nature and housing, keeping in mind the preservation of the open landscape.

109059.explanation: We will maintain the 'Muziekwijzer Altena' program and expand it to all primary and secondary schools.

109060.explanation: Sports clubs are more than just places to exercise: they are the social engines of our community. We believe they deserve support so that they can continue to play a connecting role in the future.

109063.explanation: Enforcement can take various forms. Both authorized officers and more supportive roles can contribute to safety and livability. What is appropriate depends on the local needs.

109064.explanation: When all other options are exhausted, such as investing in social presence and prevention, targeted enforcement may be appropriate. However, safeguards for residents' privacy must be in place.

109066.explanation: A starter's loan helps young buyers take the step to own a home and prevents them from being pushed out of the housing market. This way, the municipality promotes equal opportunities and affordable housing for everyone wanting to start.

109069.explanation: Legalizing holiday homes is not a solution to the housing crisis. More quality housing needs to be built. Holiday parks are intended for recreation.

109071.explanation: Social assistance is not a swamp of sanctions and obligations, but a springboard to participation in society. The main goal of social assistance is participation and the ability to join society. This can be through work, caregiving, or additional education, but not as a 'reciprocal obligation'.

109073.explanation: More funding for poverty reduction and simpler regulations. No one should have to live in poverty. Therefore, we open minimum provisions to households with income up to 130 percent of the minimum wage. For households with children, we set the limit at 150 percent of the minimum wage.

109076.explanation: Preferably, car and bicycle traffic are separated, but this is often not possible in built-up areas. Then the speed limit is reduced to 30 km/h for more safety, fewer traffic victims, and better quality of life.

109077.explanation: People should preferably be able to choose care that matches their faith, culture, or beliefs, but this must not come at the expense of the quality of care provided.

109079.explanation: We find a limited increase of the property tax justified to maintain the level of services in Altena. We will explore how to make it income-dependent, so those who can pay more carry the heaviest burden. We do not shift costs to future generations.

109083.explanation: We make Sunday openings possible for entrepreneurs who wish to do so.

109084.explanation: We are hospitable in Altena and provide a home for status holders, as agreed with the province.

109092.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, rooms can be an appropriate additional form of housing. We allow this when the quality of living, safety, and livability are well safeguarded. This way, individual rooms contribute to more housing without compromising a pleasant living environment.

109094.explanation: We want to offer tailor-made solutions here. However, we note that more parking spaces cause people to use cars more often and take away space for greenery, cycling, and playgrounds. It is better to keep villages cozy and sustainable.

109096.explanation: We are against the construction of this large church. Its size and intensive use do not fit Dussen and have too much impact on the living environment. A careful spatial assessment shows this development is not desirable here.

109097.explanation: In the past 10 to 20 years, far too much for young people has disappeared in Altena. More nightlife options give young people safe places to meet, have fun, and develop, contributing to a lively and social municipality.

109098.explanation: When building new homes or carrying out large-scale maintenance in the neighborhood, we plant extra trees and parks (according to the 3-30-300 principle: 3 trees visible from your window, 30 percent canopy cover, green within 300 meters) and create facilities for birds and insects.

109099.explanation: Accessibility for residents of Altena is inadequate; without a car, you can hardly get anywhere, and that can and must be improved! Additional bus lines make it easier to travel sustainably and independently, reducing the need for cars and making the municipality more livable.

109043.explanation: There must be room to develop activities as long as they do not disturb others. However, the government must also do its best to allow farmers to make a living from their primary business without being forced to do many additional things.

109045.explanation: It causes too many extra traffic movements and attracts additional labor migrants who need to be housed.

109046.explanation: It is not necessary, but we are not opposed to it. Asylum seekers also deserve humane shelter.

109047.explanation: Labor migrants deserve humane living conditions but must not be too dependent on their employer.

109049.explanation: We follow national policy, which increasingly imposes stricter requirements on quality and heating behavior.

109051.explanation: Without fireworks shows, a fireworks ban is untenable.

109055.explanation: One single house should not be a problem on larger plots, but that then should not apply only to farmers. Multiple houses constitute project development and the municipality must maintain control over that.

109059.explanation: Subsidies are for people who cannot afford things themselves. However, the municipality should give extra encouragement to music lessons.

109060.explanation: Financial contributions are not automatic. In special or unforeseen circumstances, the municipality can provide financial assistance.

109063.explanation: There are enough enforcement officers; however, stricter action must be taken in unsafe situations.

109064.explanation: We are not in favor of a surveillance society.

109066.explanation: More subsidies for starter loans drive up housing prices, which is an undesirable effect.

109069.explanation: Living in holiday parks regularly leads to unsafe and socially undesirable situations.

109071.explanation: People should have the opportunity to do socially meaningful work, but making this mandatory for welfare benefits is not a solution. Even people with paid jobs should do socially meaningful work, but that does not always happen.

109073.explanation: Even with an income of 120-130 percent of the minimum wage, there is still a lot of poverty.

109077.explanation: Identity-based care is especially essential for psychosocial support services.

109079.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must be linked to ambitions. Promising not to increase it is an empty promise.

109084.explanation: This is a statutory task that we must perform properly.

109092.explanation: Room rental quickly leads to undesirable situations. Renting out one or two rooms by the main resident is no problem; subdividing entire houses into rooms is undesirable.

109094.explanation: We must continue to invest in shared mobility and better public transport.

109096.explanation: The Christian Union is not opposed to (large) churches.

109097.explanation: Operating nightlife venues is not a municipal task. But where possible, the municipality should sufficiently facilitate private initiatives in this regard.

109098.explanation: Counteracting heat stress in new construction is essential.

109099.explanation: Maintaining bus lines that are not used makes no sense. However, the municipality must continue to focus on mobility solutions suited to our rural area and keep lobbying the province to allocate their public transport budgets to our municipality as well.

109043.explanation: If a store or campsite does not cause disturbance, for example to other farmers, then space should be provided for it. However, this must not come at the expense of other entrepreneurs in the rural area.

109045.explanation: The municipality should not necessarily oppose the establishment of distribution centers, but they must fit into the Altena landscape with its open countryside. VVD Altena believes it is important that entrepreneurs can take steps under the right conditions and in the interest of the wider community.

109046.explanation: For the benefit of our residents, we would preferably not want a reception center for asylum seekers. However, there is a national challenge in sheltering asylum seekers. Altena is part of the Netherlands, so if the national government obliges us, we will cooperate.

109047.explanation: Labor migrants are needed for our local economy. However, our condition is that our residents do not suffer from this. VVD Altena opposes high concentrations of labor migrants and wants them to live close to their employer, with numbers appropriate to the nearby village.

109049.explanation: VVD Altena believes everything must be seen in the right proportions. It is fine if residents enjoy a cozy campfire, but of course, we do not approve of irresponsible residents burning waste causing nuisance to others.

109051.explanation: Our municipality is made up of many villages. We think a fireworks show mainly benefits a small group of villages, while everyone has to pay for it. We find that unfair. Finally, it does not fit the core tasks of the municipality.

109055.explanation: VVD Altena finds it important that more homes are built for our residents. We also think it is important that necessary permits are granted as efficiently as possible. But we cannot give people free rein to build houses just anywhere. Farmer or not: ensure a proper process.

109059.explanation: VVD Altena believes the municipality should limit spending to its core tasks. Music lessons are nice and great but are an extra that residents should provide for themselves. This way, we keep municipal finances under control and ensure financial peace for residents.

109060.explanation: VVD Altena values health and community gatherings but sports clubs are basically independent organizations with their own responsibility for income generation and cost control. VVD Altena wants to prevent automatic reliance on the municipality.

109063.explanation: Safety in Altena is not in order. There is too often, for example, drug trafficking and production and undermining. We must take action by putting more enforcement officers to work for our safety. We prefer enforcers over policy officers.

109064.explanation: There are hotspots in Altena where drugs are sold and other crimes are committed. VVD Altena wants to combat this with, among other things, stricter camera surveillance. This is part of a broader plan.

109066.explanation: A starter loan sounds sympathetic, but it only drives prices up further and saddles people with more debt. VVD Altena wants to eliminate all friction in the housing market to ensure affordability and availability.

109069.explanation: VVD Altena prefers to keep matters separate. If something is a holiday home, it should be used as such. But we will not make things difficult immediately if a holiday home provides a temporary solution. However, it is not a permanent solution.

109071.explanation: VVD Altena finds it important that people participate in society. If you can benefit from welfare through society, it is proper to give something back. It also ensures that people will more quickly pick up a regular job when they work on their skills.

109073.explanation: VVD Altena believes it is important that people have their basic needs met. But then we want to keep the incentive as high as possible to encourage people to rejoin social life and take care of themselves.

109076.explanation: VVD Altena does not want to blindly limit everything to a maximum speed of 30 km/h. Our focus is on safety. If safety requires it, we will reduce the maximum speed as much as necessary. If it's not needed, we prevent limiting residents unnecessarily.

109077.explanation: VVD Altena grants all residents freedoms as long as they do not come at the expense of the freedoms of others. We prevent the municipality from bearing costs for specific residents with particular demands that are not generally necessary. This helps us keep expenses under control.

109079.explanation: VVD Altena wishes all residents financial peace of mind. We support an efficient, small government. This includes low taxes. The property tax (ozb) will only be raised with inflation, and if that increase is unnecessary, we will omit it.

109083.explanation: VVD Altena believes shopkeepers should be free to choose whether to open on Sundays. The hospitality sector already has this freedom. Why not for shopkeepers? We will abolish the mandatory Sunday closure and leave the choice to the retailer.

109084.explanation: VVD Altena believes that allocation of social rental housing should be fair and well-considered. That does not mean refugees automatically get priority, but their interests must be fairly weighed.

109092.explanation: VVD Altena finds it important to limit regulations concerning the housing market. For example, if someone with a modest income wants or needs to leave home at a young age, it helps to keep some flexibility in the rules.

109094.explanation: VVD Altena looks closely at parking needs within each community. If there is a shortage, we want to address it per area. This way, we avoid creating unnecessarily many parking spaces and incurring unneeded costs.

109096.explanation: VVD Altena believes it is important that there is broad support before realizing such a project. As soon as the municipality notices very little support, it must use available resources to counter this and protect the interests of residents.

109097.explanation: VVD Altena values that young people can enjoy their youth, including within the municipality. The popularity of entertainment venues is often commercially measurable. If it is not financially viable, the municipality should avoid investing in something with insufficient demand.

109098.explanation: VVD Altena believes it is important that people have cooling options on warm days and that homes are built for the long term. Nature and shade are good means to achieve this. It is wise housing policy to include nature and shade in building plans.

109099.explanation: VVD Altena finds it important that people have good mobility because that creates opportunities. At the same time, bus lines must be well utilized to justify costs. VVD Altena closely monitors how demand develops to adjust supply accordingly.

109043.explanation: This differs per situation, but an alternative destination must be possible.

109047.explanation: No disguised dependency on bed, bath, and bread. These matters should be kept separate.

109059.explanation: The subsidy faucet must be reviewed.

109060.explanation: Everyone must stand on their own feet.

109063.explanation: Fewer enforcement officers (boa's)! The huge legal cost apparatus must be significantly reduced. Residents are not cash machines or small children. Service levels must increase rather than more control!

109066.explanation: The municipality is not a bank. Guaranteeing loans might be a better choice.

109073.explanation: Don't look at income limits, but at spending limits.

109077.explanation: This has nothing to do with providing care.

109079.explanation: If necessary, this must be demonstrated and NOT because the municipal apparatus cannot control costs.

109083.explanation: Respect the environment, do not seek differences but preserve the peace of already busy daily life. Is that not possible on Sundays?

109094.explanation: Every plan falls short, we've been saying that for four years.

109098.explanation: Nature must always be included, but people take precedence over nature.

109099.explanation: There is still much to improve here!

109043.explanation: Farmers must have room to run their businesses and make them future-proof. Side activities such as a shop or campsite strengthen rural areas, increase earning capacity, and keep Altena livable. Fewer rules, more freedom.

109045.explanation: The municipality should not block upfront but strictly assess location, scale, and impact. Distribution centers can provide jobs and economic value, provided they fit the landscape, infrastructure, and local interests.

109046.explanation: An asylum seekers' center brings systematic insecurity, nuisance, and pressure on facilities. Altena is not a reception region. The municipality must protect its residents and choose peace, safety, and cohesion.

109047.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for their own staff. Housing should not be at the expense of housing and liveability for our own residents.

109049.explanation: We retain natural gas, wood stoves, and other heating methods without pressure or bans. We oppose forced connections and disconnections of existing systems.

109051.explanation: Fireworks are a tradition of residents, not a task for the municipality. Public funds should not go to central fireworks displays. Let people celebrate New Year's Eve as they wish.

109055.explanation: Farmers must have the freedom to build homes or tiny houses on their own land for family, succession, or staff. This strengthens rural areas, prevents depopulation, and breaks through unnecessary spatial rules. Fewer rules, more trust.

109059.explanation: Music education contributes to the development and creativity of children. With targeted subsidies, every primary school child gets a chance to discover talent: investing in Altena’s youth.

109060.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health, youth, and social cohesion. The municipality supports them and actively seeks cooperation. This way, we encourage children and young people to engage in sports.

109063.explanation: We use enforcement for supervision and service delivery, not to generate income through fines.

109064.explanation: Camera surveillance should not become a standard measure. It can be used in specific locations, but freedom and privacy remain the starting point. Safety begins with social cohesion and visible police presence, not by installing cameras everywhere.

109066.explanation: Starter loans solve little. The real obstacles are nitrogen regulations and the compulsion to switch off gas, which make building expensive and slow. Remove these barriers, build more homes, and truly help starters.

109069.explanation: If holiday homes are safe and suitable, people should be allowed to live there permanently. This quickly increases housing supply without new construction. Less paternalism, more housing freedom for Altena residents.

109071.explanation: Those who can work should give something back to society. This strengthens self-worth and involvement. Understanding and humane assessment are needed for those who cannot participate due to health or circumstances.

109073.explanation: The current income threshold is sufficient. The municipality should provide targeted help where it is truly needed, not keep expanding support.

109076.explanation: A general 30 km/h rule does not work. Without proven and clear safety effects, we will not impose speed limits or add extra speed bumps. Traffic requires targeted solutions, not the same measure everywhere.

109077.explanation: We want to guarantee free choice of doctor. The patient decides with whom to receive treatment. Care must be based on trust and personal conviction, without compulsion or restrictions from the municipality.

109079.explanation: Raising the property tax (ozb) directly affects homeowners, tenants, and entrepreneurs. In times of high expenses, the municipality must be frugal and efficient, not pass costs on to residents. Taxes down, not up.

109083.explanation: If there is ongoing or increasing debate about Sunday rest, we want to organize a local referendum so residents can express themselves directly. When there is no clear support for change, stores remain closed on Sundays, as has always been the principle.

109084.explanation: Priority for status holders increases waiting times. Social rental housing is meant for the local community. Residents of Altena who live, work, and contribute here should be given priority. This preserves social cohesion, shortens waiting times, and keeps villages livable.

109092.explanation: Room rental can provide extra living space, especially for starters and singles. The municipality should allow this with clear rules to prevent nuisance and protect neighborhood livability.

109094.explanation: Sufficient parking spaces are needed for all residents and visitors. They ensure accessibility of shops, amenities, and homes and keep villages livable. Parking policy should be practical, not restrictive.

109096.explanation: The municipality must be neutral and carefully weigh based on spatial integration, traffic pressure, livability, and support. Not blocking beforehand, but also no automatic approval. Decisions should be substantively and locally justified.

109097.explanation: The municipality must create space for safe and responsible meeting places for young people. This strengthens the bond with villages and community and helps prevent nuisance. Targeted investment works better than forbidding or looking away.

109098.explanation: Within built-up areas, we keep greenery well-maintained and accessible. On the edges, nature is given space to grow. We plant more trees for shade and cooling and restore hedges and fences in rural areas. Nature belongs to good building.

109099.explanation: Good accessibility is essential for livable villages. Extra bus lines, with better connections between centers, help residents reach work, school, and care. Public transport must be practical and reliable, not further cut back.

statements.title: The municipality of Altena should stop the construction plans for a large church in Dussen.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or campsite at their farm.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Altena should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality of Altena should invest money in extra bus lines.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: In Altena the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Altena moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Altena.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality of Altena should invest in more entertainment options for young people.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to live in the Netherlands. The term can mean that right itself, but also the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow shopping on Sundays.
All plans for new construction must incorporate nature and shade.
